title: Tethering
body: We increasingly have more devices that move with us, the essential mobile phone, the laptop or netbook, and soon we won't be able to live without a tablet PC like the iPad. These devices don't reach their full potential if they're not connected to the internet, and telephone operators have realized this and have "sabotaged" free Wifi networks so that we end up contracting their 3G data plans, which is really a technology that works very well but is very expensive for us, especially if we want to connect multiple devices directly or with USB 3G modems or mifis. The solution is to have only one device connected to the internet and pay a single fee, we always carry the mobile with us and converting it into a wifi router is not technically complicated. **Android** will offer it in its version 2.2 (Froyo), both via Wifi connection and USB cable [![](http://www.celularis.com/wp-content/uploads/2010/05/android22tether.jpg "Android tethering")](http://www.celularis.com/software/android-2-2-tendra-usb-tethering-y-wifi-hotspot.php) **iPhone OS 4** seems to only offer it via USB or bluetooth and, knowing Apple, it will only be accessible from other devices of the apple brand. Especially designed for those who own an iPad (what will happen with those who bought the 3G model?) [![](http://www.nikosmaridakis.com/images/IMG_0001.PNG "Android tethering")](http://forums.macrumors.com/showpost.php?p=9927307&postcount=40)[![](http://www.nikosmaridakis.com/images/IMG_0002.PNG "Android tethering")](http://www.celularis.com/software/android-2-2-tendra-usb-tethering-y-wifi-hotspot.php) Unfortunately it is expected that operators will have this option disabled on subsidized devices, or as seems to be the case with iPhone, they will activate it for an additional payment.
